reviews.review_title: Can not download photos into gallery
reviews.review_description: Apparently, it’s a known issue. Since I seen more recent reviews saying it worked, I thought I would give it a try, but I’ve downloaded it reinstalled restart it both app phone, and headset. I’m still unable to view any photos in my gallery. I can say the limited selection that they have in their own gallery on this app when testing it out, you can customize the size the contrast so you can see and the pivot scale as if a canvas was slightly tilted. As a shame, I was really looking forward to using this ￼￼

Good App. It's hard to draw small details, but it's probably because of bad camera in Quest 3S. Upload own template IS WORKING , you just need to copy your images to "Download" folder (connect your device to PC via USB and copy file).

reviews.review_title: Great way to map out pictures
reviews.review_description: First time using this was out of this world Im use to printing out picture  then grinding up then carefully drawing out .Then rubbing out grid .this does away with all of this it’s revelation and ups my game and opens endless possibilities 👍👍👍
Images are synced, yet they are not showing up in the app when I go to use it. That’s the reason I bought this so I didn’t have to use the projector anymore. Not happy

This was really easy to use! It only took some very minor fiddling to get it positioned on the canvas. Adding my own photos was a simple process.Adjusting the transparency was easy. MUCH easier to use than a projector for sketching my art. My only suggestion to the devs (if feasible) is that the options are to project vertically or horizontally, but flat in both instances. That's probably great for muralists who are drawing on walls! But for a canvas on an easel, it needs to be at a slight angle. Not a huge deal and it still worked, but it would be cool if the calibration could include a third point to take the tilt of the canvas into account. Overall, a great app and one that I will be using for more art in the future!

developer_response.body: Thanks! I've put your suggestion for canvases on the to-do list, it will be included in the next bigger update

reviews.review_description: everything is good about it except for one thing you should be able to change it to where you can decide if it's see through wish so that way you can see what you're drawing but right now it's all thing and whenever i try and trace it with my pencil i always mess up and i have to put it above and i have to try and sketch it out but so far so good hope you guys make it see through we're in the settings where you can make it see through what are on the menu so that way you can actually trace it and actually be better at drawing.

It appears to be a known issue (or at least in the reviews) that you can't upload images.  Problem still hasn't been fixed.  Downloaded the latest version, restarted device.  App is unusable until this issue is resolved.
Does not work and does not show up what you've drawn very disappointing 😑

developer_response.body: Hi! You can pull the right thumbstick down to reduce the opacity of your image

reviews.review_title: Not letting me upload my own images
reviews.review_description: I’m so upset because it’s not letting me upload my own images and I need a drawing for homework. Y’all need to fix that ASAP

developer_response.body: Hi! I uploaded a hotfix. I hope it's not too late for your assignment.

reviews.review_description: Working proof-of-concept, but nothing more. Yet.
Awesome

Just got a metaquest3s and I want to sketch somthing out for my GF. All permissions and images are “on/available￼” and I’ve restarted the device but it says “no images in gallery” so it seems to not be working currently.

reviews.review_title: Might try it out again after improvement
reviews.review_description: Tried this out today, it's a bit weird. image doesn't stabilize enough in my experience .Was able to trace the item however. Might try it out again after improvements.
Bad
free no subscription unlimited image upload

It is free, so i give 3stars. 
Not stable.

If it would work on the new update i would give it a higher raiting
cant accses anything in my dounloads

developer_response.body: The upload system has been reworked. Images inside the gallery should be detected automatically now, and uploading should be a lot easier

reviews.review_description:
If you could pin a corner, and then set / orient each corner would be a gamechanger. It’s just so difficult to align.
Second point, have a place to upload your image to other than having to download it from a webpage.
I installed, downloaded a pircture, uploaded it in the app and started drawing. worked perfectly instantly ^^

Horizontal tracing worked pretty fine. The picture trembled a little, but got the job done. Vertical tracing was a little tricky to calibrate at first. I'll have to try that again. Needs some improvements, but otherwise very promising and useful app :)

I have a quest 2 and I got the app and tried downloading it until I seen that it was fo a quest 3 and so I tried deleting it but it’s not letting me delete the app. Also it would be fun to play if it was for quest 2 or more.

developer_response.body: Hi! Your not being able to delete the app is unfortunately a Meta-related issue, there is nothing that can be done on TraceArtist's side. Sidequest has an older version of the app if you like to try it, it's available for every headset.

reviews.review_description: Ver y good to impress you friend or family and very fun

reviews.review_title: Great start to AR drawing app
reviews.review_description: Very easy to use, library of images is growing but you can upload your own. Works well in AR. Can’t wait to see where the devs take this app, the sky is the limit!
It was great but the people that made this should add were you can upload your own image but other then that every thing is good and works great

developer_response.body:
Hi! Glad you liked it. You can already upload your own images.
Here is a short, 2 minute guide on the topic:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=htpSoPIQfD8

reviews.review_description: Just tried uploading my own image and it worked. So easy to use. I was a bit confused on how to adjust the image transparency settings but once I figured it out it was easy to use and adjust. The ability to download an image and see it in the app is a really useful feature. I can only imagine the app getting better from here. Great job on the app.

From the first try I have drawn Van Gogh sunflowers on my kitchen wall! And it is GOOD! 
Just one thing - please make it possible to move the image a little further from the hand, I literally lacked a couple of centimeters to place it evenly on the wall. 
Im very surprised really, so simple and so useful app!
Please add a camera function to the app so you can take a photo of an object with the VR headset camera and immediately get its image in the app.
Used it for a large painting. I wish I could quickly toggle the reference image on and off. Sliding opacity up and down is nice, but makes it hard to spot differences when refining final image.
loving this, one very desirable engancement would be to have a way of auto re-syncing with your real drawing after taking a break, can be a real ballache trying to realign them

developer_response.body: Noted, will be added next update

reviews.review_description: It is in early access and free. This is a really great resource for artists, I spent 20 mins and was able to roughly trace one of the detailed preloaded images of a building. I have uploaded a couple images to trace tmrw and it was simple and easy. Setting up your image and paper properly is important, it seems easiest to do with a single sheet of paper on a flat surface.
Doesn't propeprly align with surface and can't see pen or what you've drawn through the image

developer_response.body: Please grab the image and move the thumbstick down, you will then start seeing your pen strokes and everything else. Align the image by grabbing and placing it where you want. If that doesn't work, send me an email at traceartistapp@gmail.com

reviews.review_description: I have a big national mushroom event coming up and it's Halloween weekend. I was able to download images of pumpkins and pumpkin carving stencils with mushrooms off the browser, easily import them and then turn up the transparency to trace the lines onto paper and make free flawless patterns to carve! Best pumpkins I've ever carved! What a cool use of MR! Thank you

I really love the concept, but it’s a lot harder than it seems.  If you’re going to draw something with a bunch of lines, like a castle, it’s easy to get lost in your drawing.  While the projected AR image stays steady, the real world does not through the pass through camera.  So many times I find that areas that I drew shift around.  Meaning the line that I drew is no longer overlaid with the projected image.  The other gripe I had was trying to place the projected image directly over your drawing surface.  You have to literally have your controller touching the projected image to position it.  Well, if your paper is 3 or 4 inches from the table side, it’s impossible to have the image placed directly above the paper without gaps in between.  In order to get the projected image directly above the paper without gaps in between, you need to have your paper at the very edge of the table…. If only there was a way to position the projected image from a distance rather having to virtually touch the image with the controller.

Despite the negative points, this is a great tool to draw out simpler cartoon characters like Mickey Mouse, Bluey, or Doraemon.  But keep in mind the more detail the harder it is.
Imagines distort when moving 1cm. It’s a horrible experience.

developer_response.body: Hi, the distortion you're seeing in passthrough is a limitation of the Quest's camera system (warping). Unfortunately, individual apps can't improve this as it's handled at the system level.

reviews.review_description: It will not work cuz I can not see my pencil

developer_response.body: You have to decrease the image opacity first (grab the image and move the thumbstick down). If that doesn't work, send me an email at traceartistapp@gmail.com
